text:
A Dutch child rapist competing in Paris has broken down in tears over his hostile welcome to the Olympic Games, complaining about the crowd’s constant boos.

Convicted child rapist and beach volleyball player, Steven van de Velde, received a less than warm reception at the Paris Olympic Games, breaking down in tears after being booed by the crowd.

He was 19 at the time.

The child had added the beach volleyball player on social media after he reportedly commented on one of her photos.

A court was told Van de Velde was aware of the girl’s age when he went to her home when her mother was out and took her virginity.

It was reported that Van de Velde cried in the trial when it was revealed his victim had since self-harmed and overdosed after he raped her.

He was released in 2017 after serving only 12 months of his four-year sentence.

Upon his release, he said raping the girl did not make him a "paedophile" or a "sex monster".

Breaking down in tears, Van de Velde revealed he had doubts about attending the Olympics after intense media security erupted following his qualification.

“I definitely thought about (skipping) it, yes," he said.

“I did something wrong, ten years ago. I have to accept that."

Van de Velde, now married with a two-year-old child, blasted the extensive media coverage of his conviction.

“Hurting people around me - whether it’s Matthew, my wife, my child … that just goes too far for me.

“That’s definitely a moment where I thought, is this worth it?”

Van de Velde was quickly rising through the ranks in beach volleyball, and at the time of the offence was considered one of the best in his country, representing the Netherlands at the 2015 European Games.

His conviction did little to slow his sporting career and he has continued to succeed since being released, returning to international competition in 2018.

Recently he joined forces with Matthew Immers, for the Paris Olympics, who defended his partner.

“He’s had his punishment. And now he’s really kind," Immers said.

The Netherlands Olympic Committee released a statement in the lead-up to the games, revealing Van de Velde would not be staying at the Olympic Village alongside other athletes, and would have no media contact during his stay in Paris.

"Van de Velde has consistently remained transparent about the case which he refers to as the most significant misstep of his life," the committee's statement read.

"He deeply regrets the consequences of his actions for those involved.  He has been open about the personal transformation he has undergone as a result."

The committee said it "regrets the impact of the unforeseen renewed attention, on social media in particular, for those struggling with trauma from sexual offences and transgressive behaviour."

keywords: 2024 Paris Olympic Games,Steven Van de Velde
